data.title: Paul Mueller GAAP EPS of $14.73, revenue of $94.14M
data.description:
• Paul Mueller press release (MUEL): Q4 GAAP EPS of $14.73.
  • Revenue of $94.14M (+33.5% Y/Y).

data.title: Turbo Energy expects FY 2025 revenue between $22.5M and $23.5M; shares jump 13%
data.description:
• Turbo Energy (TURB) on Monday issued a shareholder update highlighting preliminary, unaudited revenue results for fiscal year 2025 with expected revenue in the range of $22.5M and $23.5M, representing 130%-140% year-over-year growth compared to FY 2024 revenue of $9.77M. 
  • Shares of TURB  jumped over 13% during pre-market trading to $2.84.
  • "We further strengthened our strategic positioning through key validations and partnerships, including UL certification, proprietary technologies supported by patents, collaboration with leading global technology partners, and major industrial contracts, such as Pamesa Grupo Empresarial. Fiscal year 2025 revenue (unaudited) includes approximately $10 million in contracted value associated with projects executed with IM2 Energía Solar in connection with jointly developed energy solutions for electro-intensive industrial clients, subject to customary revenue recognition criteria. In these projects, Turbo Energy provides advanced energy storage systems and intelligent energy management solutions," said Mariano Soria, chief executive officer.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Air Canada CEO Michael Rousseau to retire
data.description:
• Air Canada (AC:CA) on Monday said that Michael Rousseau will retire by the end of the third quarter of 2026, after nearly two decades of leadership.
  • Rousseau will continue to lead the company and to serve on its board until that time.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Uber to buy chauffeur service firm Blacklane
data.description:
• Uber Technologies (UBER) has agreed to acquire chauffeur service platform, Blacklane.
  • The acquisition is subject to the receipt of customary regulatory approvals and satisfaction of other customary closing conditions and is expected to close by the end of 2026.
  • Founded in Berlin, Germany, in 2011, Blacklane connects guests worldwide with independent local chauffeur services via an app and web booking platform. The company operates in over 500 cities across more than 60 countries.
  • After the transaction is complete, the acquisition of Blacklane will further accelerate Uber’s recently-announced move into the chauffeur sector with Uber Elite.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Standard Lithium reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Standard Lithium press release (SLI): cash and working capital of $152.3 million and $147.6 million, respectively, as of December 31, 2025.
  • The company has no term or revolving debt obligations as of December 31, 2025.

data.title: Bold Ventures secures Ontario grant for Burchell metals project
data.description:
• Bold Ventures (BOL:CA) on Monday said it has been approved for a grant of C$212,500 from the Ontario Junior Exploration Program for its Burchell Base and Precious Metals Project in Ontario.
  • The project is located about 100 km west of Thunder Bay.
  • The funding is part of a broader C$10 million provincial investment across 68 exploration companies announced by Ontario’s Ministry of Energy and Mines.
  • The company said a second phase of drilling at the Burchell project has been completed, with six holes totaling more than 800 meters, and samples are being prepared for analysis.
  • BVLDF closed +179.2% at $0.0349.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Chevron says Wheatstone repairs to take weeks, further tightening global LNG supply
data.description:
Chevron (CVX) said Sunday that its Wheatstone liquefied natural gas facility in Western Australia is unlikely to ​resume full production for several weeks as it repairs damage caused ‌by last week's tropical cyclone, adding another blow to the global LNG market still reeling from the halt of operations in Qatar.

"While damage assessments ​continue at both the onshore Wheatstone plant and offshore Wheatstone Platform, it is ​likely to be a number of weeks before production returns to full rates to allow time for repairs to be safely completed," a Chevron (CVX) Australia spokesperson said.

The Wheatstone plant accounted for 2.4% of global LNG trade in February, shipping 11 cargoes — with 10 going to Japan and one to Thailand, according to advisory EnergyQuest.

The cyclone was estimated to have disrupted Australian LNG supply equating to more than 30M metric tons/year; combined with the shock from the Middle East war, ​more than 25% of global LNG supply has been ⁠disrupted, MST Marquee analyst Saul Kavonic said Friday.

Wheatstone was one of three LNG plants in Western Australia whose production was affected by Tropical Cyclone Narelle late last week; Woodside Energy (WDS) said it is working to resume normal operations at its North West Shelf facility, and Chevron (CVX) said its Gorgon site was operating at full rates after an outage at one of its three production units.

Woodside (WDS) also said production has continued at its Macedon and Pluto gas facilities, and ship loading at Pluto LNG, which was not affected by the storm, is restarting following the reopening on Saturday of the Dampier port.

data.title: Virtuix signs cooperative research and development agreement with U.S. Navy
data.description:
• Virtuix (VTIX) on Monday announced it has signed a Cooperative Research and Development Agreement with the Naval Postgraduate School (“NPS”) in Monterey, California.
  • Under the agreement, Virtuix will deliver an Omni One system to NPS’s Modeling, Virtual Environments, and Simulation Institute, where researchers will examine the suitability and effectiveness of small-footprint, omni-directional navigation technology for a range of tasks in the context of military training and simulation.
  • This is Virtuix’s first formal research agreement with the U.S. Navy, the company said.
  • VTIX +0.59% premarket to $6.72.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Camtek secures $31M multi-system order
data.description:
• Camtek (CAMT) announced on Monday that it has received a multi-system order totaling $31 million from a leading OSAT mainly for CoWoS-like packaging supporting AI applications.
  • Including this order, total orders received from leading OSATs during the first quarter of 2026 have already exceeded $90 million, the company said. It added that the systems are expected to be delivered within this year.
  • Rafi Amit, Camtek's (CAMT) CEO, commented, "We are very encouraged by the exceptional volume of orders from leading OSATs in the first quarter of 2026, which underscores our observation that OSATs are playing an increasingly central role in the advanced packaging market for the AI applications industry. Camtek is uniquely positioned to support this growing demand with our advanced inspection and metrology capabilities. This order flow strengthens our confidence in our continued business momentum and a strong second half of 2026."
  • CAMT +2.39% premarket to $157.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Expedia and Instacart gain after being called AI beneficiaries by Jefferies
data.description:
Jefferies sees upside for Expedia (EXPE) and Instacart after recent developments suggest the Internet names could be AI beneficiaries. 

Analyst John Colantuoni and his team upgraded both to a Buy rating as they called the recent sell-off a buying opportunity. The positive view on the AI upside for the names is based in part on OpenAI's (OPENAI) shift away from consumer-facing products and its plans to scale back direct checkout ambitions in favor of advertising. In addition, Google's (GOOGL) commitment to remain a source of referral traffic is seen as a positive.

"We believe these developments support our view that LLMs will evolve into another performance channel that helps consolidate share around scaled Internet players. In addition, we see AI helping improve recommendation engines, reduce customer acquisition costs, enhance product velocity, and lower customer service costs," advised Colantuoni.

Jefferies set a price target of $300 on Expedia (EXPE), implying a ~14X 2027 GAAP P/E trading multiple. The price target on Instacart (CART) was set at $45, which works out to a 15X 2027 GAAP P/E. 

Shares of Expedia (EXPE) rose 2.7% in premarket trading, while Instacart was up 1.4%.
